question: The ionic radii of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mo>+</mo></msup><mo>,</mo><msup><mi>Na</mi><mo>+</mo></msup><mo>,</mo><msup><mi>Al</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Mg</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> are in the order :
solution:
Al3+,Mg2+ and Na+ are isoelectronic ionic species. For monatomic ionic isoelectronic species as positive charge increases ionic size decreases.

The order of size of Na+& K+ is Na+<K+,

∴ Increasing order of ionic radii:  Al3+<Mg2+<Na+<K+

options:
f is continuous at every point in [0,∞) and
differentiable except at the integer points.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi></math> is both continuous and differentiable except at the integer points in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>[</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mo>∞</mo><mo>)</mo></math>.

solution:
f:[0,∞)→[0,∞), fx=∫0xydy

Let x=n+f,f∈(0,1)

⇒x=n, x=f.

So

fx=∫01ydy+∫12ydy+∫23ydy+…+∫n-1nydy+∫nn+fydy

fx=∫010dy+∫121dy+∫232dy+…+∫n-1nn-1dy+∫nn+fndy

fx=01-0+12-1+23-2+…+n-1n-n-1+nn+f-n

fx=0+1+2+…+n-1+nf

⇒fx=n(n-1)2+nf

=[x]([x]-1)2+xx

Note limx→n+fx=nn-12

limx→n-fx=(n-1)(n-2)2+n-1

=n(n-1)2

fx=nn-12n∈N0

so f(x) is continuous ∀x≥0 and differentiable except at integer.'

question:
Let y=y(x) be solution of the following differential equation 

eydydx-2eysinx+sinxcos2x=0, yπ2=0.

If y0=logeα+βe-2, then 4(α+β) is equal to            .
solution:
Let ey=t⇒eydydx=dtdx

So,

eydydx-2eysinx+sinxcos2x=0

⇒dtdx-2sinxt=-sinxcos2x

So, the integrating factor I.F.=e∫-2sinxdx=e2cosx

So, the solution of differential equation is 

t·e2cosx=∫e2cosx·-sinxcos2xdx

⇒ey·e2cosx=∫e2z·z2 dz  (Assume, z=cosx⇒dz=-sinx)

Using integration by parts,

⇒ey·e2cosx=12·z2·e2z-12z·e2z+e2z4+C

⇒ey·e2cosx=12·cos2x·e2cosx-12cosx·e2cosx+e2cosx4+C

at x=π2,y=0⇒C=34

⇒ey=12cos2x-12cosx+14+34·e-2cosx

Put x=0

⇒y=log14+34e-2⇒α=14,β=34

So, α+β=14+34=1

y=±23,x=1

There exists a one-one function f:N→N such
that fog=f

solution:
g:N→N

g(3n+1)=3n+2

g(3n+2)=3n+3

g(3n+3)=3n+1

gx=x+1;x=3k+1x+1;x=3k+2x-2;x=3k+3

ggx=x+2;x=3k+1x-1;x=3k+2x-1;x=3k+3

gggx=x;x=3k+1x;x=3k+2x;x=3k+3

If f:N→N,f is a one-one function such that f(g(x))=f(x)⇒g(x)=x, which is not the case

If f:N→N, f is an onto function such that f(g(x))=f(x) one possibility is

fx=n;x=3n+1n;x=3n+2n;x=3n+3; n∈N

Here fx is onto, also f(g(x))=f(x)∀x∈N

question: A spherical gas balloon of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>16</mn></math> meter subtends an angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo>°</mo></math> at the eye of the observer <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> while the angle of elevation of its center from the eye of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>75</mn><mo>°</mo></math>. Then the height (in meter) of the top most point of the balloon from the level of the observer's eye is :
solution:
Assume, O is the  centre of sphere

and P,Q are the point of contact of tangents from A

Let T be top most point of balloon & R be foot of perpendicular from O to ground.

From triangle OAP, sin30°=16OA

⇒OA=16cosec30°=32

From triangle ARO, sin75°=OROA

⇒OR=OAsin75°

⇒32(3+1)22=86+82

So the height (in meter) of the top most point of the balloon from the level of the observer's eye is OR+OT

=8(6+2+2)

solution:
Given hyperbola is

16x2-9y2+32x+36y-164=0

⇒16x+12-9y-22=164+16-36

⇒16x+12-9y-22=144....1

⇒(x+1)29-(y-2)216=1

Compare the above equation with ⇒(x-h)2a2-(y-k)2b2=1

We get, h=-1, k= 2, a2=9 and b2=16

∴ Eccentricity, e=1+169=53

We know that, focii are h+ae, k and h-ae, k

Hence, focii are (4,2) and (-6,2)

Let the centroid be (h,k) & A(α,β) be point on hyperbola

So h=α-6+43, k=β+2+23

⇒α=3h+2, β=3k-4

Put the values of α,β in the equation1,

163h+2+12-93k-4-22=144

⇒144(h+1)2-81(k-2)2=144

⇒16h2+2h+1-9k2-4k+4=16

⇒16h2-9k2+32h+36k-36=0

So, the locus of the centroid is 

16x2-9y2+32x+36y-36=0

solution:
(p⇒q)∧(q⇒~p)

=(~p∨q)∧(~q∨~p)  {p⇒q=~p∨q}

=(~p∨q)∧(~p∨~q)  {commutative property}

=~p∨(q∧~q)   {distributive property}

=~p

solution:
Let X=abcd & A=0i10n

⇒AX=IX

⇒A=I

⇒0i10n=I

⇒A8=1001

⇒n is multiple of 8.

So number of 2 digit numbers in the set 

S=16,24,32,…,96

Clearly, 16,24,32,…,96 are in arithmetic progression.

Here, a=16, d=24-16=8 and l=a+n-1d=96

Where, a is the first term, d is the common difference, l is the last term and n is the number of terms.

Consider, l=a+n-1d=96

⇒16+n-18=96

⇒n-18=80

⇒n-1=10

⇒n=11

Hence, number of two-digit numbers in set S is 11.
